Courier Sprite.description: Courier sprites excel at securely delivering messages and are also effective trackers.

Crack Sprite.description: Crack sprites specialize in stealthy Matrix operations and are particularly suited to quiet intrusions that need to remain under the radar.

Data Sprite.description: Data sprites are masters of finding and manipulating information, making them excellent librarians, search agents, and data specialists.

Fault Sprite.description: Fault sprites are combat specialists: tenacious digital fighters designed to shred hostile icons and protect their technomancer in a Matrix fight.

Machine Sprite.description: Machine sprites are the Core Rulebook sprites most closely associated with the physical world, acting through devices and specializing in electronics.

Camouflage.description: Conceals a file inside another file so that normal Matrix searches cannot find it. The hidden file can only be discovered with a Matrix Perception Test specifically looking for it, and even the sprite must make that test to locate and extract the file.

Cookie.description: Tags a target persona with a hidden cookie file that records its Matrix activity. The cookie runs silent, has protection equal to the sprite's Level, and can record hosts entered, communications metadata, programs used, and similar activity. Net hits determine the depth of the resulting report. At a chosen time, the cookie transfers its data back to the sprite; if the sprite is absent from the Matrix at that moment, the file is deleted.

Diagnostics.description: Lets the sprite evaluate an electronic device and assist someone using or repairing it as a Teamwork Test. If the sprite scores at least one hit, the assisted character gains +1 to the applicable limit, and each hit adds 1 die to the character's dice pool. The sprite must devote its full attention to maintaining the bonus.

Electron Storm.description: Engulfs a target persona in a sustained barrage of corrupting datastreams. On the initial successful attack and on each later action the sprite takes while sustaining the power, the target suffers Resonance DV Matrix damage, resisted normally. The target also suffers 2 points of noise. All Electron Storms end immediately if the sprite takes any Matrix damage.

Gremlins.test: Hardware + Level [Attack] v. Device Rating + Firewall
Gremlins.description: Causes a targeted device to malfunction. A successful test inflicts a glitch appropriate to the device and circumstances. With 4 or more net hits, the result is treated as a critical glitch.

Hash.description: Temporarily protects a file with a unique Resonance algorithm that only the sprite can remove. The protection lasts while the sprite carries the file, for a maximum of Level × 10 Combat Turns. If the sprite stops carrying the file, it returns to normal; if the sprite is destroyed while carrying it, the file is permanently corrupted and becomes worthless.

Stability.description: Protects a persona or device on which the sprite has a mark from ordinary malfunctions and accidents, including normal glitches and those caused by Gremlins or Accident. Standard glitches are ignored and critical glitches are reduced to normal glitches.

Suppression.description: While the sprite is inside a host and sustaining this power, any IC launched by that host is delayed by Level ÷ 2 Combat Turns. Delayed IC cannot act or be targeted until it finishes launching.

Watermark.description: Places an invisible Resonance marking on an icon that only Resonance-driven entities can see. It can be used to leave secret messages on Matrix objects, can overwrite an existing watermark, and remains until the icon ceases to exist or the watermark is removed with Erase Matrix Signature.

category.Sprites:
Sprites
Sprites are Resonance entities compiled by technomancers. A sprite's Level (L) determines its Device Rating, Resonance, Matrix attributes, Initiative and Matrix Condition Monitor.

Compiling
Choose a sprite Level up to twice the technomancer's Resonance and make the Compile Sprite Resonance action. Each net hit gives the technomancer one task from the sprite. Compiling causes Fading equal to twice the sprite's hits on its defense test, with a minimum DV of 2. The Fading is Physical if the sprite's Level exceeds the technomancer's Resonance; otherwise it is Stun. A technomancer can have only one compiled sprite at a time.

Tasks
A task can cover one use of a sprite power, one Combat Turn of Matrix actions directed toward the same job, or participation in a cybercombat until the enemies are defeated or the sprite and its owner escape. A remote task causes the sprite to return to the Resonance when the job is complete, forfeiting any remaining tasks.

Registering
A compiled sprite may be registered to extend its usefulness and gain access to registered-sprite tasks. The process takes a number of hours equal to the sprite's Level. Registered sprites do not count against the one-compiled-sprite limit, and a technomancer may have registered sprites up to their Logic attribute.

Sprite Statistics
All Core Rulebook sprites have Device Rating = Level, Resonance = Level, 4D6 Initiative Dice, and a Matrix Condition Monitor of 8 + (Level ÷ 2) boxes.
